recommended_fix: enforce+teach: validator REJECTS path-like (`/`) or tuple-like (`(`,`,`) identifiers in ADR `Domain(s)` AND spec `## Impacted Domains` with an error that teaches the ADR-0032 short-tag contract at point-of-use; the path->tag decision is routed to the operator. lola migrates to durable, disjoint short-tag domains; spec-100 path resolution stays but is NOT extended to the ADR side.
rationale: 6ou2 is a namespace seam, not a missing-resolution bug: spec 100 resolved Impacted-Domains->short-tag but left the ADR `Domain(s)` field as free-form literals, so lola's path/tuple labels (`api (lola, tools)`, `api/app/lola/`) never intersect the resolved name `spec056`. Fix (a) 'resolve the ADR side through OWNERSHIP' is the trap: those Domain strings aren't declared globs -- to glob-resolve them the framework must heuristically decide a free-form string IS a path, strip trailing slashes, ignore parenthetical tuples. That is exactly the free-form heuristic ADR-0036 forbids, and its path-overlap variant reintroduces the `internal/foo` vs `foo` ambiguity ADR-0032 explicitly rejected. Fix (b) bakes the domain==spec anti-pattern and a catch-all multi-owner trap into the schema. Fix (c) hollows the gate for everyone. The ZFC-canonical move is enforce+teach: a deterministic STRUCTURAL check on declared data -- does a Domain/Impacted token contain `/` or `(`? -- reject with an error that teaches the short-tag contract, routing the path->tag decision to the operator (mirroring ADR-0036's stub+prompt pattern; cf. the existing `viz/agentmind/bench` first-segment hard error at ownership.go:282). Detecting a separator is syntactic, total, no guessing; deciding what a label OWNS stays with the agent. mindspec's own short-tag ADRs are unaffected.
adr_action: Amend ADR-0032 sub-decision 1: re-affirm the short-tag as the single canonical identifier across all THREE artifacts, and add the explicit reject+teach rule for path-like/tuple identifiers in the ADR `Domain(s)` field (and Impacted Domains). Scope the existing spec-100 amendment as an Impacted-Domains-ONLY one-way convenience that does NOT license path-like ADR Domain literals; note path-overlap was considered and rejected as reintroducing the ambiguity sub-decision 1 already rejected. No ADR-0036 amendment needed -- enforce+teach IS ADR-0036 applied (deterministic check on declared data + cognition routed to operator); optionally cross-reference it from ADR-0032. This is a bug-fix-with-governance-clarification, not a new spec.

findings.issue: Fix (a)'s 'resolve the ADR side through OWNERSHIP' requires heuristically parsing free-form ADR `Domain(s)` strings (`api (lola, tools)`, `api/app/lola/`) into paths/globs -- the exact free-form-string heuristic ADR-0036 forbids. The path-overlap variant reintroduces the `internal/foo` vs `foo` ambiguity ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 explicitly rejected. Path-overlap is ZFC-UNCLEAN because the ADR side declares no globs at all -- there is nothing to overlap without first guessing the string is a path.

Asymmetric namespace is the root cause: spec 100 normalized Impacted-Domains->name but left ADR `Domain(s)` and OWNERSHIP dir-name as the canonical identifier. The intersect (intersectFold/domainSliceContains, plan.go) compares a resolved name against raw ADR literals -> always false. North star must be ADR-0032's enforce-short-tags, NOT spec-100's accommodate-paths direction extended to the ADR side.

Fix (b) `:: spec` keyword bakes the domain==spec anti-pattern and a catch-all multi-owner trap into the OWNERSHIP schema, and expands the validator's interpretive surface ('claims paths under the umbrella of spec X') -- more framework semantics, not less. It papers over the free-form ADR literals without fixing them.

lola's catch-all OWNERSHIP (name: spec056) claiming every touched tree monopolizes shared paths; it only works today because the other domain's globs are dead (trailing-slash, no `/**`). The next spec touching those trees hits normalizeImpactedDomains' >1-owner hard ERROR (ownership_resolve.go:152-154). Disjoint short-tag domains under enforce+teach removes this latent trap.

Enforce+teach hard-errors lola's current path/tuple ADR Domain lines (intended -- lola is in ADR-0032 contract violation). mindspec's own ADRs already use short-tags (core/execution/validation/adr/lifecycle/workflow/ownership) and are unaffected. lola must re-tag domains + ADR Domain lines + Impacted Domains to durable short-tags.

Spec 100's path->name resolution was a partial drift toward accommodating path-like identifiers, but it is itself ZFC-clean (resolves via EXPLICIT declared globs to exactly one owner, hard-errors on zero/many, no prefix inference). Keep it as an Impacted-Domains convenience; do NOT extend the same path-thinking to the ADR side. Reconsidering/capping spec 100's scope is the governance follow-up, not a rollback.

fixes_assessed.a: reject -- 'resolve the ADR side' forces heuristic parsing of free-form `Domain(s)` strings into paths (ZFC violation per ADR-0036); path-overlap reintroduces the `internal/foo` vs `foo` ambiguity ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 explicitly rejected.
fixes_assessed.b: reject -- `:: spec` umbrella keyword bakes the domain-as-spec anti-pattern and a multi-owner catch-all trap into the OWNERSHIP schema and grows validator semantics; it accommodates the violation instead of fixing the free-form ADR literals.
fixes_assessed.c: stopgap -- error->warning is ZFC-neutral but hollows the ADR-0032 gate for EVERY repo (drift resumes compounding); acceptable only as a temporary unblock while lola re-tags, never the destination.
